methods.description: Synthesis of NMC811 and NMCA materials using oxalate co-precipitation method.

steps.description: To synthesize NMC811, the solution described in Table 1 was dripped over a period of 4hrs into a solution comprising 240 mmol oxalic acid dissolved in 400 ml of a 1:1 mixture of H2O:EtOH, under constant stirring and at a temperature of 60 °C.
steps.searchable_summary: drip metal cation solution into oxalic acid solution under controlled conditions

steps.description: To synthesize NMCA, solutions 1 and 2 as described in Table 1 were dripped successively into a similar oxalic acid solution under the same reaction conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: drip NMCA solutions into oxalic acid solution under controlled conditions

steps.description: After precipitation, the suspensions were aged 20 h under stirring before solvent evaporation.
steps.searchable_summary: age suspensions under stirring before evaporation

steps.description: The resulting powders were ground and calcined at 450°C for 5 h.
After calcination the powders were reground and fired at 800°C for 20 h under flowing O2 (5 Nl/h).
steps.searchable_summary: regrind and fire powders at 800°C under O2 flow

steps.description: The as-fired NMC811 and NMCA products were ground and sieved to 40 µm.
steps.searchable_summary: grind and sieve products to 40 µm

steps.description: SiO2-decorated NMCA was prepared by treating the calcined NMCA powder with 2 wt% (3-Aminopropyl)triethoxysilane (APTES) at 80°C in EtOH for 20 h.
steps.searchable_summary: treat NMCA with APTES in EtOH at 80°C

steps.description: The silane treated powder was dried and annealed as described above.
steps.searchable_summary: dry and anneal silane treated powder

methods.description: Characterization of phase purity and morphology of synthesized samples using X-ray diffraction and scanning electron microscopy.

steps.description: Phase purity and morphology of the samples was analyzed by powder X-ray diffraction using a Bruker D8 Advance A25 diffractometer, with data fitted using the Bruker Topas v5 software.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze phase purity and morphology using X-ray diffraction

steps.description: Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) was carried out with a FEI APREO scanning electron microscope, equipped with an Oxford Instruments Aztec energy dispersive x-ray spectroscopy (EDS) system.
